articles.title: French aircraft carrier group prepares for mission in Hormuz Strait
articles.summary: French aircraft carrier group prepares for mission in Hormuz Strait The French Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier group is heading into the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden as part of preparations by France and the UK to navigate vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.  The French Ministry of Armed Forces said in a statement that the group crossed the Suez Canal on Wednesday. "The movement of the naval task force is separate from the military operations initiated in the region and complements the existing security arrangements," it added.

articles.title: Trump Will Address China Buying Iranian Oil, Greer Says

articles.title: US and Iran finalising 14-point memorandum to end war: Report
articles.summary: US and Iran finalising 14-point memorandum to end war: Report The US and Iran are moving closer to a one-page ​memorandum to end the war in the region, two sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.  "We will close this very soon. We are getting close," said one source from Pakistan, which is currently acting as a mediator between the two countries.  A 14-point, one-page memorandum would officially end the war, followed by talks regarding transit through the Strait of Hormuz and the US lifting sanctions on Iran, as well as discussions on Iran's nuclear programme.

articles.title: IRGC announces 'safe and stable' transit through Hormuz with the end of US threats
articles.summary: IRGC announces 'safe and stable' transit through Hormuz with the end of US threats Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy (IRGC) said on Wednesday that with US threats coming to an end, "safe and stable" transit through the Strait of Hormuz will be ensured. Citing the statement made by the IRGC, state media indicated that new procedures would be put in place, but did not specify what they could entail.  The IRGC also thanked owners and captains of vessels in the Gulf for respecting Iranian regulations when moving through the waterway.

articles.title: Iran struck over 228 US structures, equipment across region: Report
articles.summary: Iran struck over 228 US structures, equipment across region: Report Iranian air strikes have damaged or destroyed at least 228 US structures or equipments across the region since the start of the war in late February, the Washington Post reported. Using satellite imagery, the daily indicated that some of the attacks hit hangars, barracks, fuel depots,  radar systems as well as communications and air defense equipment.  "The amount of destruction is far larger than what has been publicly acknowledged by the U.S. government or previously reported," the report added.&nbsp
